lines.speaker: SPEAKER_01
lines.text: Hi, I'm Jeff, uh product manager at Square Satoshi.

lines.speaker: SPEAKER_01
lines.text: Also have Breno here, uh one of our uh... uh leader uh developers uh he'll be assisting with uh presentation portion about the code assistant.

lines.speaker: SPEAKER_01
lines.text: But yes, we've been hard at work um building some large language models, and uh we are a bit bitcoin centric um yeah company now I guess uh a little bit of a background context.

lines.speaker: SPEAKER_01
lines.text: As you know, there's a lot of different models out there.

lines.speaker: SPEAKER_01
lines.text: Uh you know, Google OpenAI Meta, kind of the leading companies in the industry right now, uh, but there are some inherent problems or challenges there.

lines.speaker: SPEAKER_01
lines.text: Um we've seen some very uh uh public facing examples of that recently with uh kind of the catastrophe of Google's recent large language model release.

lines.speaker: SPEAKER_01
lines.text: Uh but we are here to build some models that have a little bit different of a bias in them.

lines.speaker: SPEAKER_01
lines.text: Um some people are concerned with having a model that has any kind of a bias and think that they should be completely neutral, but the reality is that's just not possible to do.

lines.speaker: SPEAKER_01
lines.text: Um every training data set is gonna have a specific bias in it.

lines.speaker: SPEAKER_01
lines.text: So our goal is to present uh large language model that does not have a fiat bias baked in, uh, but instead uh presents more of a Austrian economics uh bitcoin centric uh emphasis to the world.

lines.speaker: SPEAKER_01
lines.text: So but today um we'll give a little bit of a background uh about code Satoshi, why we built it, um, and then also a more generic view of uh first off, like what we were building before we got into that.

lines.speaker: SPEAKER_01
lines.text: So uh first off, um, can you guys see my screen?

lines.speaker: SPEAKER_01
lines.text: We can.

lines.speaker: SPEAKER_01
lines.text: That's great.

lines.speaker: SPEAKER_01
lines.text: Okay, yeah.

lines.speaker: SPEAKER_01
lines.text: So we actually started uh building uh just

lines.speaker: SPEAKER_01
lines.text: And uh building uh just our main Satoshi model, which is just a I guess you could call it generic, but just a uh a chap for learning and education about Bitcoin generally.

lines.speaker: SPEAKER_01
lines.text: Uh we also released uh Satoshi GPT in the GPT store uh that you can go and find today.

lines.speaker: SPEAKER_01
lines.text: And then on our website, uh we have uh an open source uh fine-tuned Lama213b that's powering that today.

lines.speaker: SPEAKER_01
lines.text: So this enables you to just go and ask any question about a bit more Bitcoin generally.

lines.speaker: SPEAKER_01
lines.text: Uh really helpful for sending your normie friends to it uh who ask you questions like you probably if you've been in Bitcoin a while, been used to answering the same questions over and over again about Bitcoin, uh, which is fine.

lines.speaker: SPEAKER_01
lines.text: Like orange pulling people is is fun and exciting, but it's uh hard to scale.

lines.speaker: SPEAKER_01
lines.text: And so this is a really good tool that we can share with the world to help educate people about Bitcoin, um, and doesn't tell them to you know diversify and buy Ethereum and stuff like that.

lines.speaker: SPEAKER_01
lines.text: So uh Satoshi GPT, we also gave it some really cool capabilities, like you can ask it all types of questions about the Bitcoin price, uh estimating the halving, finding Bitcoin merchants, summarizing the news, building charts, uh some really cool stuff that we gave it the ability to do.

lines.speaker: SPEAKER_01
lines.text: Um so yeah, go ahead and hop in uh to our website and you can play around with that.

lines.speaker: SPEAKER_01
lines.text: It's spiritual satoshi.ai.

lines.speaker: SPEAKER_01
lines.text: Um, or in the GPT store, you can find uh Satoshi.

lines.speaker: SPEAKER_01
lines.text: Uh but what we also found in um this process is that and and with any MLM is it requires you to gather a lot of data, but then also to build and structure that into a very clean data set that needs to be used to to train the model and to iterate on.

lines.speaker: SPEAKER_01
lines.text: Um that also requires some instruction fine-tuning and evaluation, like you need to be able to actually assess if what you've done uh achieves a specific outcome, especially because the data sets involved are just so large that it's almost like

lines.speaker: SPEAKER_01
lines.text: Involved are just so large that it's almost impossible to evaluate it with uh human eyes.

lines.speaker: SPEAKER_01
lines.text: You know, getting to tens or hundreds of thousands of data points, you need to have uh an automated way to evaluate that.

lines.speaker: SPEAKER_01
lines.text: Uh, but this also requires uh human feedback as well, um, because any kind of automated evaluation is gonna have some limitations to it.

lines.speaker: SPEAKER_01
lines.text: So what we wanted to do is be able to crowd source uh uh the knowledge of the bitcoin community.

lines.speaker: SPEAKER_01
lines.text: Uh we call it proof of knowledge, and so what we built is uh a lightning enabled crowdsourcing LLM tool.

lines.speaker: SPEAKER_01
lines.text: So it allows Bitcoiners to come in uh to receive uh a data point.

lines.speaker: SPEAKER_01
lines.text: Uh often that's just a question and answer pair, and they can use their knowledge to help us curate and clean and annotate this data so that we can use that in training.

lines.speaker: SPEAKER_01
lines.text: And it's uh it's a really cool opportunity because it means that Bitcoiners all over the world can get involved um and be able to earn stats for the knowledge that they've gained learning about Bitcoin, and in the process help us to build and fine-tune a model that helps share that knowledge with the world.

lines.speaker: SPEAKER_01
lines.text: And Bitcoin micropayments is really what's made that possible.

lines.speaker: SPEAKER_01
lines.text: I mean, there's there's other like AI data sourcing tools out there, but they have the inherent limitations of fiat, which is like cross-border payments and small transaction sizes.

lines.speaker: SPEAKER_01
lines.text: So uh leveraging Bitcoin, we've actually been able to make a tool that improves the knowledge of the Bitcoin model as well.

lines.speaker: SPEAKER_01
lines.text: Um what we did though is we actually started talking to a lot of companies about how they can leverage um um AI to help their company, and what we found uh specifically with the liquid community is liquid community is that there was a need there for developers.

lines.speaker: SPEAKER_01
lines.text: Um, some of the feedback we got with our conversations.

lines.speaker: SPEAKER_01
lines.text: There's just a lot of uh uh it's a complex uh industry, complex technology, um, and it's challenging for a lot of developers to get to get started, especially because uh it's just a newer technology, there's not um you know extensive uh uh uh documentation or knowledge sharing that's out there yet because it's so new.

lines.speaker: SPEAKER_01
lines.text: Um, and so a lot of the companies we talked to just expressed some of the challenges they were having, and so we we found that there's a really good uh opportunity here to help uh developers in the space uh uh get started more quickly, and so that's kind of what started our our desire to build a liquid and uh code assistant tool.

lines.speaker: SPEAKER_01
lines.text: Um, so yeah, it's uh very useful.

lines.speaker: SPEAKER_01
lines.text: Um it's been proven out there with tools like Copilot, which has been uh adopted heavily by developers, that these can be effective tools for saving time, and saving time is uh saving money because engineers are one of the largest expenses for uh uh modern tech companies.

lines.speaker: SPEAKER_01
lines.text: But the problem is is that um there's not Bitcoin centric code assistance out there.

lines.speaker: SPEAKER_01
lines.text: So if you go and ask existing um large language models like Copilot or something, very specific questions about Bitcoin script, mini script, uh liquid development.

lines.speaker: SPEAKER_01
lines.text: Uh sometimes they'll do okay, but often they they they they they they they they they they they just hallucinate or or fall flat on their face, they just make things up or they just don't know the answers.

lines.speaker: SPEAKER_01
lines.text: Uh and especially as this ecosystem grows, there'll be uh just a greater need for incoming engineers to get up to speed more quickly.

lines.speaker: SPEAKER_01
lines.text: Um, and that's what we're hoping to do is to help bring the thousands of new bitcoin engineers that will need to enable hyperbitcoinization uh and give them the tools they need to to do so quickly.

lines.speaker: SPEAKER_01
lines.text: Um yeah, and then also in in addition to the actual mini script code assistant, uh, we also have developed a liquid assistant, which helps to explain uh Bitcoin and liquid related documentation.

lines.speaker: SPEAKER_01
lines.text: Um instead of just spending time like trying to pour through lots and lots of documentation here, you could just quickly ask a question.

lines.speaker: SPEAKER_01
lines.text: You know, there's uh some existing tools that liquid developers use to interact with uh industry experts.

lines.speaker: SPEAKER_01
lines.text: Um, but that's just there's just limited time there uh for people to help out.

lines.speaker: SPEAKER_01
lines.text: And here's a tool that you can just ask at any time.

lines.speaker: SPEAKER_01
lines.text: Um instead of having to search for documentation, you just ask your question, you can get the answer in minutes uh instead of uh... uh the hours it might take to pour through uh documentation or wait for our assistance.

lines.speaker: SPEAKER_01
lines.text: Uh so we have those two, yeah.

lines.speaker: SPEAKER_01
lines.text: We have the liquid assistant and then uh the code Satoshi.
